Open_Ended_Feedback: I love the collaborative culture here. The grade-level team meetings are incredibly valuable. I wish we had more planning time built into the schedule.
The PD opportunities have been fantastic this year. The coaching model really helps me grow. Compensation is my biggest concern.
Lincoln Innovation Academy is the best school I've ever worked at. Dr. Rivera truly cares about teachers and students alike.
As a first-year teacher the support has been good but I feel overwhelmed by the number of initiatives. Would benefit from more focused priorities.
Great team and school culture. The intervention block scheduling needs improvement - too many transitions for young students.
The curriculum alignment work we did last summer has really paid off. I feel more confident in what I'm teaching and why.
Caseload is very high this year. I appreciate the admin support but we need another SpEd teacher or paraprofessional desperately.
Similar to my colleague - the caseload situation is unsustainable. Love the school but feeling burned out.
This is a special place. The science lab renovation has made a huge difference in what I can do with students.
The mentorship program for new teachers is excellent. My mentor has been incredibly generous with her time.
Been here since the beginning and the growth has been remarkable. Ready for the next chapter of strategic planning.
The literacy initiative is well-designed. I appreciate that we got input into the curriculum adoption process.
The math curriculum needs updating for the upper grades. Students arrive with very different preparation levels.
I wish specials teachers were included more in PD planning. Our needs are different from core content teachers. Love the creative freedom though.
Great school community. The wellness initiative has been a nice addition. Facility needs for PE are adequate but could be better.
Dr. Rivera has been very supportive of the arts program. Would love more budget for instruments and materials.
This role allows me to make a real impact. The coaching cycle structure gives me enough time with each teacher.
Enjoying building the STEM coaching program. Need more time for data analysis to target coaching priorities.
The SEL curriculum implementation has gone well. Student needs are increasing and we could use additional counseling support.
Love working here. The restorative practices training has been transformative. Seeing real changes in how students handle conflict.
Strong grade-level team. Would appreciate more autonomy in selecting supplementary materials.
Transition from 5th to 6th grade is tough for students. Need better systems to support that shift. Good school overall.
I feel valued by teachers but wish there were clearer career pathways for paras. Compensation is a concern.
Enjoy working with students but the workload has increased significantly without corresponding support or pay increase.
Great school to work in. Teachers treat me as a real partner. Benefits package could be improved.
Dr. Rivera runs a tight ship but always with warmth. Best admin team I've worked with in education.
The enrollment management system upgrade has made my job much easier. Good communication from leadership.
Respectful work environment. Building is well-maintained. Summer deep clean schedule works well.
Would like more involvement in school wellness decisions. Kitchen equipment needs updating.
The early childhood program at Lincoln is truly special. Parent engagement in K is outstanding thanks to the family liaison.
Solid school with a clear mission. The project-based learning emphasis sets us apart. Need more field trip budget.
STEM focus is strong in upper grades. Students are well-prepared for high school science. Lab supplies could be better funded.
Good school that values technology integration. Device refresh cycle needs to be formalized. One person covering IT and EdTech is a stretch.
This role is so meaningful. Families trust us because we show up consistently. The multilingual communication improvements have made a big difference.
Wonderful first year. The onboarding process was thorough and I felt ready on day one. Looking forward to year two.
